<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by cmd/docgen — do not edit by hand -->
# lgtm plugin

Manages pull request approvals via slash commands. Tracks approval count against a configurable minimum and maintains a GitHub check run.

## Triggers

GitHub `issue_comment` and `pull_request` webhook events.

## Slash commands

### `/lgtm` / `/approve`

Approves the pull request. Adds the `lgtm` label, removes `do-not-merge`, and marks the LGTM check run as passed.

```
/lgtm
```

### `/remove-lgtm` / `/remove-approve` / `/remove-approval` / `/unapprove` / `/unlgtm`

Revokes approval of the pull request. Removes the `lgtm` label, adds `do-not-merge`, and resets the LGTM check run to neutral.

```
/remove-lgtm
```

## Pull Request lifecycle events

| Action | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `opened` | Adds `do-not-merge` label; creates LGTM check run with `neutral` conclusion. |
| `reopened` | Adds `do-not-merge`, removes `lgtm`, posts an approval-reset comment, and creates an LGTM check run with `neutral` conclusion. |

## Labels

| Label | Meaning |
|-------|---------|
| `lgtm` | PR has been approved. |
| `do-not-merge` | PR is not ready to merge. |
